Adjustment based on other adjustment methods:
Adjustment based on modernization (adjustment measure)
Adjustment based on index-linked rent
Adjustment by entering graduated conditions
Adjustment using combined methods
The rent adjustments and the reserve are calculated in the same way as the
general rent adjustment method.
3.3.8  Integration with Footprint Assessment
Business Background
SAP S/4HANA supports the integration with a footprint assessment solution (currently SAP Sustainability
Footprint Management) to allow you to consider product footprint information available in this footprint
assessment solution in your business decisions.
Key Features
When a footprint assessment solution is integrated, SAP S/4HANA supports you to access product footprint
information in the footprint assessment solution.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

3.4
Human Resources
3.4.1  Human Resources (HR) Administration
Business Background
HR administration enables you to manage personnel administration tasks: from storing all types of information
about your employees to basic personnel actions such as hiring or reassigning employees. Moreover, employee
and manager self-services enable you to increase productivity and reduce operational burdens for HR staff.
3.4.1.1
Employee Administration
Business Background
Employee administration enables you to record, maintain, and protect relevant employee data from
unauthorized access. With employee administration, you can trigger and perform personnel actions, such
as hiring a new employee.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage and Process Employee Data
You can use this feature to enter and process employee
data within your company. This includes actions such as
hiring a new employee, reassigning an employee to another
organizational unit, or reassigning an employee to another
country/region.
Determine Employment Periods
You can use this feature to determine and calculate different
employment periods for your employees. You can define, for
example, whether a new employment period starts when an
employee returns to your company after having left.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Set Up Authorizations
You can use this feature to set up general authorizations
which are based on the SAP-wide authorization concept
or to set up HR-specific authorizations which check if a
user is authorized to perform an activity depending on the
organizational assignment.
Integrate with ArchiveLink
You can use this feature to use the storage functions
provided by ArchiveLink to facilitate the use of external
storage systems to outsource copies of incoming
documents such as employment contracts.
Support Process Automation to Change Master Data
You can use this feature to define and execute automated
HR processes in which changes are to be made to HR
master data. Such a process could be an employee
requesting reduced working hours which would entail a
change of the employee's time management data. This
feature also allows to decentrally execute HR activities as
it includes managers and employees (where necessary)
directly in the process flow and thus reduces the workload
for the HR department. Moreover, HR specialists can also
be involved in the process for checking the accuracy of data
or for adding additional data before data changes are stored
on the data base.
3.4.1.2
Global Assignment Management
Business Background
Global assignment management enables you to transfer employees or potential employees from one location
to another in order to perform the required job responsibilities.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage Global Employees
You can use this feature to manage expatriation and
repatriation processes. This includes the planning and the
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
preparation of the global assignment as well as the global
assignment itself, and the transfer back to the employee's
origin.
Manage Concurrently Employed Employees
You can use this feature to manage HR master data,
time data, and benefits of your concurrently employed
employees. Moreover, you can use this feature to run the
payroll for these employees.
3.4.1.3
Employee Connectivity
Business Background
Employee connectivity involves the transmission of employee master, organizational, and cost-center data
between enterprise systems.
Key Features
You can find the key features in the chapter Integration with External HR System [page 54].
3.4.2  HR Strategy and Planning
HR strategy and planning enables you to use HR data to measure and improve workforce-related outcomes,
and continuously provide informed reporting to enable workforce decisions. Additionally, you can use
this component to assess future workforce to minimize potential gaps and to prioritize future workforce
investments.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

3.4.2.1
Workforce Analytics
Business Background
Workforce analytics enables you to use quantitative and qualitative data to measure and improve workforce-
related outcomes, and provide analyses to enable workforce decisions.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
HR Reporting
Various, pre-defined standard reports and key figures are
available to process HR data.
Analyze HR Data
You can analyze HR data from different points of view
depending on your requirements using individual reports.
3.4.2.2
Workforce Planning
Business Background
Workforce analytics enables you to use quantitative and qualitative data to measure and improve workforce-
related outcomes and provide analyses to enable workforce decisions.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Features
Use
Plan and Control Budgets
You can use this feature to manage budgets by monitoring
the use of funds and by managing the funds available
according to position plans.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Features
Use
Plan and Simulate Personnel Costs
You can use this feature to plan the development of your
employees' wages and salaries and the benefit costs such
as employer contributions to social insurance and further
education. Moreover, this feature enables you to simulate
different planning scenarios and to analyze the effects these
planning scenarios might have.
3.4.3  Learning Management
Business Background
Learning management allows you to define, create, and convey structured information and knowledge
associated with a curriculum. It enables you to track course progress and to analyze learning deficits.
Moreover, employee and manager self-services enable you to increase productivity and reduce operational
burdens for HR staff.
3.4.3.1
Training Management
Business Background
Training management enables you to define and create adapted training for your employees in different
formats depending on your and your employees' needs.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage Trainings
You can use this feature to manage and plan courses
including determining training demands and course
scheduling as well as the administration of courses
including participation and resources, billing and fees.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Find and Participate in Courses
You can use this feature to browse a course catalog and
find courses that suit the learning needs of your employees.
These courses include classroom and web-based trainings.
Moreover, this feature enables you to book, rebook, or
cancel courses, see what's planned and track the course's
progress.
Create Web-Based Learning Content
You can use this feature to design and structure new
instructional content, and to integrate learning content
from external providers.
3.4.4  Organizational Management
Business Background
Organizational management allows you to build, maintain, and reorganize your company's structures of
the workforce by creating a hierarchy that mirrors your organizational and reporting structures. Using
organizational management, you can get a clear picture of your organization, its structure, plan, and individual
units depicting positions, jobs, and employees, for example, or users which are assigned to these positions.
Organizational management thus enables you to analyze your company's structures and, based on this, allows
you to plan workforce requirements. Moreover, employee and manager self-services enable you to increase
productivity and reduce operational burdens for HR staff.
3.4.4.1
Organizational Structure Management
Business Background
Organizational structure management enables you to build and maintain organizational structures within your
company and to enhance these structures with your own entities, such as organizational units. This provides an
overview of your functional and reporting structures which allow you to analyze the resource planning.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage Organizational Structures
You can use this feature to create organizational units,
and to build organizational structures by assigning the
organizational units you created to each other.
Moreover, you can use this feature to edit and display
organizational structures, and to delimit and delete
organizational units.
Enhance Organizational Structures with Your Own Entities
You can edit organizational plans by enhancing the
organizational structure with any structure (for example
additional reporting structures) including object types
(such as team, group, division) you have defined yourself.
3.4.4.2
Position Management
Business Background
Position management enables you to create and maintain position information which triggers HR processes
when actions are required.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Edit Positions, Jobs, Tasks, and Staff Assignments
You can use this feature to maintain and update position,
job, and task information, and to assign or reassign
employees to positions to update the position information.
By assigning employees and by adding attributes to a
position, you can get a clear picture of your reporting
structure.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

3.4.5  Payroll and Reimbursement
Business Background
Payroll and reimbursement enable you to collect and prepare the necessary information to pay employees
accurately and on time, and to comply with payroll regulations and statutory reporting requirements for
employees. Moreover, employee and manager self-services enable you to increase productivity and reduce
operational burdens for HR staff.
3.4.5.1
Payroll Management
Business Background
Payroll management helps you to ensure accurate and on-time payments for your employees.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage Gross Payroll
You use this feature to determine an employee’s basic remuneration as well as
any special payments, overtime payments, or bonuses that must be effected
for a certain period.
Manage Net Payroll
You use this feature to determine employees net remuneration according to
contractual requirements. Net pay is the amount paid after the deduction of
net and social insurance contributions from the gross remuneration.
Manage Off-Cycle Payroll
As an addition to regular payroll, which carries out payroll at fixed regular inter-
vals, this feature enables you to carry out specific off-cycle payroll activities for
individual employees on any day.
Manage Loans
You use this feature to create, administer, and perform payroll for company
loans in the SAP system.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage Bank Transfers
You use this feature after the payroll run to generate data medium exchange
(DME) files for your bank. Based on these files, the bank can process the wage
and salary payments.
Post to Accounting
You use this feature to evaluate the payroll results for posting to accounting, as
the evaluated payroll results contain information that is required by account-
ing.
Control Payroll Processes
You can use this feature to plan, monitor, and audit your payroll process, and to
verify the data quality whenever there’s a change to employee data.
Manage Pension Funds
You can use this feature to manage a pension fund and to calculate employee
contributions to such a pension fund.
3.4.5.2
Reimbursement Management
Business Background
Reimbursement Management allows you to reimburse the travel expenses by securely transferring settlement
results to Financial Accounting, Payroll Accounting, or Controlling Funds Management.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Reimbursement Amount Calculation
You use this feature to calculate the reimbursement amount of your travel
expense based on a per diem or flat rate for accommodations, meals and legs
of a trip, or based on individual receipts.
Reimbursement Settlement
As soon as the trip is approved, the settlement program starts automatically,
triggering the reimbursement process and posting, based on the trip data
provided and the calculated reimbursement amounts.
Reimbursement Transfer
You use this feature to transfer calculated travel reimbursements, depending
on the method of payment chosen, to Financial Accounting, Payroll Account-
ing, or Controlling Funds Management.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Reimbursement Payment
You use this feature to pay out the actual reimbursement amount either by
check, with the next payroll or by secure bank transfer to the business travel-
er's bank account. The travel expense results are always transferred to Finan-
cial Accounting for posting.
3.4.6  Rewarding
Business Background
Rewarding enables you to manage benefits your company offers in order to retain employees. It provides an
overview of the available benefits plans and of the eligibility for these plans. Moreover, employee and manager
self-services enable you to increase productivity and reduce operational burdens for HR staff.
3.4.6.1
Benefits Management
Business Background
Benefits Management allows you to develop, deliver, and communicate benefits packages and programs to
meet employees' health, retirement, and work-life balance needs.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage Benefits
You can use this feature to manage the benefits you
offer to your employees. This includes enrolling employees
in benefits plans, and terminating such enrollments,
monitoring the eligibility for plans, or communicating
benefits information to the plan providers.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage Country/Region-Specific Benefits
You can use this feature to manage country/region-specific
benefits. These country/region-specific benefits include for
example features for the US (administer COBRA plans,
flexible spending accounts, and tax sheltered annuity
plans), for Asia (claims), or for the UK (administer benefits
packages).
3.4.6.2
Compensation Management
Business Background
Compensation management enables you to efficiently manage compensation and budgets, and to analyze pay
practices to develop the compensation strategy that works best for your company.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Features
Use
Manage Compensation
You can use this feature to plan and process changes to
compensation, including the preparation of the compensa-
tion planning process.
Set Up Long-Term Incentives
You can use this feature to set up your company’s long-term
incentive (LTI) plans, and describe how equity compensation
should be granted and handled. This includes administering
awards, handling vesting schedules, and canceling LTI plans.
Manage Budgets
You can use this feature to create, change, and monitor
budgets in your company.
Determine Job Pricing
You can use this feature to analyze, evaluate, and determine
the compensation level for specific jobs, based on the cur-
rent market rate.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

3.4.7  Talent Management
Business Background
Talent management enables you to flexibly develop and foster talents, monitor their performances and
qualifications, and find measures to further develop their potential. You can identify and build on employees'
strengths to fill key positions in your company. Moreover, employee and manager self-services enable you to
increase productivity and reduce operational burdens for HR staff.
3.4.7.1
Career Development
Business Background
Career development enables you to provide the right tools, knowledge, and opportunities for employees to
develop their short-term and long-term career goals. It also allows you to monitor your employees' progression.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Define Development Goals
You can use this feature to define development goals. It
also enables you to add qualifications such as a first or
second language, or expertise in handling certain programs
or tools.
Define Development Measures
You can use this feature to define development measures
such as training or mentoring on how the defined
development goals can be achieved.
Track Development Goals
You can use this feature to track the progress of previously
defined development goals.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Employee goal management enables you to manage the process of setting objectives. Such processes
integrate breaking down overall enterprise objectives to the departmental level including the planning of tasks
for individual employees, checking that these tasks are fulfilled in a review, and the resulting feedback cycles.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage Objective Setting Processes
This feature enables you to manage user-defined objective
setting processes including the planning, reviewing, and
performing of the process.

Employee performance management enables you to monitor your employees' performances and to find the
best matches for key positions in your company.

You can use this feature to create groups to classify talents

meetings to discuss talents.

You can use this feature to find suitable talents from a
pool of talents to identify possible successors for important
positions or to discuss talents in talent review meetings.

your company.

Business Background
Succession management enables you to identify key jobs and positions in your company, and to make sure
that these critical roles are assumed by the employees that best fit the requirements for these jobs and
positions.
Key Features

by, for example, indicating jobs and positions as key
jobs and key positions, or by assigning career types
(such as management career) or levels (such as executive

Edit the Job Architecture
You can use this feature to edit the job architecture that
displays the job hierarchy and the relationship between jobs
and positions. This feature enables you to edit objects, such
as jobs and positions, to edit hierarchical relationships, and
to assign qualifications to objects.
3.4.8  Time Management
Business Background
Time management enables your managers and employees to manually or automatically record personnel
times. You can manage the recorded time data and optimally plan your employees' capacities. You can
also use the data provided by time management to analyze your employees' time data and monitor their
work schedules, quotas, and other time-related information such as working times. Moreover, employee and
manager self-services enable you to increase productivity and reduce operational burdens for HR staff.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

3.4.8.1
Absence Management
Business Background
Absence management allows your company to record, approve, and track employees' planned absences
related to health, family, and personal time off.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Record Absences
This feature allows you to easily record absence times, such
as leave and illness times. The recorded data can be used to
evaluate and to account your employees' absences.
Use Quotas to Monitor Absence Entitlements
You can use quotas to set up and manage time accounts to
monitor employees' absence entitlements.
3.4.8.2
Attendance Management
Business Background
Attendance management allows you to manage time-related employee information, such as the time
management status or the working hours. Based on this data, you can plan, record, and evaluate employee
attendances.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage Time Data
You can display and maintain time management data of
different types (such as working hours) which you can
change, copy, or delete.
Record Attendances
This feature allows your employees to easily record
attendance times, such as actual working times, or
deviations from an employee's general activities, such as
seminar attendances or business trips. The recorded data
can be used to evaluate and to account your employees'
attendances.
Record Time Data for Multiple Employees Simultaneously
You can maintain time data for multiple employees at the

times.

such as start and end times recorded at time recording
terminals.

Management

components such as Plant Maintenance and Customer
Service to time management.

3.4.8.4
Time Calculation
Business Background
Time calculation enables you to manage and analyze evaluated time data, such as working hours or overtime,
and allows you to prepare global payroll processes, such as managing the gross payroll, based on the data
provided by time calculation.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Evaluate Personnel Times
You can process time data that has been imported
from time recording subsystems and data that has been
recorded online. You can evaluate time data of employees
who enter only exceptions to their usual work schedule
and of employees who record all times relevant to their
working time (actual times). It also helps you to compare
the planned working time with the time recorded.
Adapt the Processing of Time Data in Time Evaluation
You can adapt the processing of time data in time
evaluation, depending on which rules time evaluation is
influenced by. Rules, such as treating attendance on a
weekday differently to attendance on a public holiday, or
differently determining overtime for hourly-paid employees
as for salaried employees, determine how time data is
processed and which results time evaluation provides.
Present Employees' Time Results
You can present the results determined by time evaluation
to your employees using time statement forms based on
company-specific templates.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage Evaluated Time Data
Time data administrators can check, correct, and display
evaluated time data. You can also perform various
administrative tasks, such as processing time evaluation
messages or providing information on planned and actual
working times.
Use Incentive Wages for Performance-Oriented
Remuneration
You can define performance-oriented remunerations, such
as time wages or premium wages. Incentive wages can be
defined for individual employees or on the level of specific
groups.
Integrate Time Data with Payroll
You can provide information on bonuses and the valuation
of hourly and overtime wages to Payroll. This information
is required for the calculation of the gross wage.It is also
possible to provide this information to third-party payroll
systems if integrated to SAP S/4HANA.
3.4.8.5
Time Tracking
Business Background
Time tracking enables you to record and manage time data effectively and efficiently. It provides you with a
comprehensive and effective business concept and related processes for manual and automatic recording of
personnel times. Time recording allows you to enter employee time data for working time, leave, business trips,
and substitutions using different methods. This data can be entered as clock times or in hours and can contain
account assignment specifications for other SAP applications.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Maintaining and Displaying Time Data Us-
ing The Time Data Menu
You can update or display time data using the Time data menu. This option
allows you to access all Time Management infotypes in the Personnel Time
Management, and then display or maintain them.
Maintaining and Displaying Time Data Us-
ing Calendars
You can maintain or display data using either a weekly, monthly, or annual
calendar. Using calendars facilitates the fast entry of data, and also allows you
to view the data record history for a selected period.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Fast Entry of Data
The fast entry of time management data enables you to maintain several
subtype records for an employee within the same time infotype, as well as
maintain one infotype for several employees. The fast entry function merely
takes required entries into account. This allows you to record essential time
management data more quickly and efficiently.
Cross-Application Time Sheet
You can use the Cross-Application Time Sheet for recording working times and
tasks. It enables you to control all business processes concerning your employ-
ees’ tasks – from paying the employees, through monitoring the progress of a
project, to creating invoices.
Personnel Time Events
The Personnel Time Events component enables you to connect external time
recording systems to SAP Time Management. If your employees enter the start
and end of their working times on time recording terminals, you can use this
component to process the resulting time events in Time Management. You can
also process employee expenditure data resulting from purchases at company
cafeterias and service stations.
Plant Data Collection
This component controls the transfer of confirmations to Time Management
from the following application components:
Production Planning and Process Control
Plant Maintenance and Customer Service
Project System

3.5
Manufacturing
3.5.1  Production Planning
3.5.1.1
Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling
Business Background
You use Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS) for SAP S/4HANA as a production planner to
create finite production plans taking into account resource schedule, component availability and order dates/
times in detail. Typically, you use PP/DS to plan your critical products and bottleneck resources.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Create proposals for in-house production
or external procurement to cover product
requirements
You can create proposals for both internal production and external procure-
ment with dates and times across multiple plants to cover material shortages.
You can create production plans either with automated production planning
run using heuristics or with manual intervention.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Optimize plan and sequence the resource
schedule and the order dates/times
You can adopt a finite plan taking resource availability and capacity into ac-
count. With a finite plan, hard constraints modeled in the system are taken into
consideration.
You can automate the optimization of the plan according to certain criteria,
such as setup times and setup costs, to achieve a finite plan or to improve the
planning situation.
You can sequence planned orders on production lines for individual lines or
for a complete line network. Your order sequence takes required dates and
restrictions into account with minimal possible violations.
You can enable continuous production, particularly in process industry by ad-
justing the duration of production without changing the order quantity.
You can sequence production operations running on a defined production re-
source taking resource availability and capacity into account. Sequencing of
orders producing materials being relevant for demand-driven replenishment
can be done using calculated priority based on projected on hand status.
Orders of products not being relevant for Demand-Driven Replenishment [page
73] can be sequenced based on priority which is calculated using remaining
time buffer.
Create proposal based on simulative plan-
ning parameters
You can create proposals for both internal production and external procure-
ment with dates and time based on simulative planning parameters, without
affecting operative planning.
Planning with Characteristics
You can use planning with characteristics where receipts are consumed with
requirements, based on matching characteristic values. You can plan or pre-as-
sign resource capacities for products with specific attributes, with the purpose
of using the capacities more rationally.
Execute and monitor production at de-
fined reporting points
You can enter and monitor production confirmation (backflushing) for planned
orders with defined reporting points.
Create a supply plan using a cost-based
optimization technique
You can create supply plans to ensure the availability of materials for a defined
part of your manufacturing or supply chain network.
The production planning tool supports multi-level planning, using a time
bucket-based planning method. It considers constraints such as production
capacity, lead time, material flow and maximum delay allowed in fulfilling a
demand. You can generate an optimized supply plan in the form of planned
orders and purchase requisitions by weighing different costs and penalties
(for example- for production, stock transfer, external procurement, storage)
against each other.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Side-by-side deployment
With the SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling capability
of SAP Digital Supply Chain Management, edition for SAP S/4HANA, you can
deploy an SAP S/4HANA system in which you use the advanced planning
features of Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS) side-by-side
with one or more SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP systems with harmonized master
data that you use for other ERP components.
The SAP S/4HANA system that you use for PP/DS is connected to the ERP
systems via Core Interface (CIF). Master data is integrated from the ERP sys-
tems to the PP/DS system, transactional data is exchanged bidirectionally. The
integration is controlled via integration models that are created and activated
in the ERP systems.
Integration with SAP IBP order-based
planning
You can use the production planning integration feature of SAP Integrated
Business Planning for Supply Chain (SAP IBP) to combine the operational sup-
ply planning features of SAP IBP order-based planning (OBP) with the detailed
planning and scheduling features of PP/DS. The integration of orders is con-
trolled by the production planning integration mode (PPIM) that is assigned to
a location material.
3.5.2  Manufacturing for Production Engineering and
Operations (PEO)
3.5.2.1
Extended Production Engineering
3.5.2.1.1
Bill of Material (BOM) Handling and Handover to
Manufacturing
Business Background
Your responsibility as a product engineer is to design new products or product lines that meet customers’
needs as best as possible. The result of this phase is the engineering bills of material (EBOMs).
As production engineer, you want to take advantage of the latest developments in process technology with the
aim of improving quality and reliability. This may require regrouping components in the manufacturing bills of
material (MBOMs) for better manufacturability, for example.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Create version-controlled bills of material
With version-controlled bills of material, you can ensure that consistent bills of
material are used in production execution. Each bill of material version has its
own release status, whereby only a released version can be used in production.
If you need to change a bill of material after it has been released, you have to
create a new version.
Create bills of material for configurable
materials
You can create version-controlled bills of material for configurable products or
materials.
Object dependencies are an important feature of bills of material for configura-
ble materials as they control which components are selected from the bill of
material (BOM) when a specific configuration of the material is manufactured.
When performing the EBOM-to-MBOM handover in the Visual Enterprise Man-
ufacturing Planner (VEMP), you have the following options for handing over
object dependencies from the source EBOM:
You can adjust object dependencies in the target MBOM to reflect any
restructuring of BOM components that you have performed during the
EBOM-to-MBOM handover.
You can edit object dependencies in the target MBOM so that they match
your specific manufacturing requirements.
You can compare and reconcile differences between the object depend-
encies in the source EBOM and the object dependencies in the target
MBOM.
Implement a BAdI to define a custom logic for the handover and reconcili-
ation of object dependencies.
Specify unitized parameter effectivity
You can create super bills of material using product unitization, whereby you
can specify the effectivity of the BOM items. As a result, when you produce a
particular unit, the super bill of material is filtered to retrieve the information
relevant for the unit that is assigned to the production order.
In addition, you may often redesign existing products and your changes may
only be valid for a certain range of end-item units. For every bill of material
item, you can specify the range of end-item units that need the component.
Product unitization is built on the parameter effectivity function to make use of
certain semantic patterns and rules to ensure:
Accurate incorporation of engineering bills of material into manufacturing
bills of material.
A meaningful match between the bill of material components and the
routing operation activities.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Define maturity levels for version-control-
led BOMs
You can define the various stages of completeness for a version-controlled
BOM and therefore control whether it is ready for production engineering, for
planning, or for both planning and execution, for example.
Specify the physical location of visual
component instances in manufacturing
bills of material
In the Visual Enterprise Manufacturing Planner (VEMP), you can add reference
designators to visual component instances in the manufacturing bill of mate-
rial to indicate where components with multiple instances are to be assembled.
If already defined in the engineering bill of material, reference designator infor-
mation is inherited by the manufacturing bill of material.
Plan assembly and disassembly of tempo-
rary parts
You can plan the assembly and disassembly of temporary parts or fixtures
within the same order or across orders. When creating the manufacturing
bill of material in the Visual Enterprise Manufacturing Planner (VEMP), you
can add non-visual components with a positive (+) or negative (-) quantity,
whereby the negative quantity in the manufacturing bill of material is treated
by SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations
(PEO) as a by-product. Alternatively, you can add a component with negative
(-) quantity when creating a bill of material directly.
Create a disassembly bill of material
You can create a disassembly bill of material for serialized products to disas-
semble a finished product with the aim of recovering parts that can be reused.
You have the following options for creating the disassembly bill of material:
You can use a manufacturing bill of material as the source bill of material
and create a disassembly bill of material containing the same components
as in the source bill of material.
You can create a disassembly template bill of material in the Visual Enter-
prise Manufacturing Planner in which you only include the components
that you want to disassemble and reuse.
Then you can convert this disassembly template bill of material into a
manufacturing bill of material for disassembly.
In both cases, during the creation of the disassembly bill of material, the sys-
tem:
Creates a new header material.
Adds the original header material of the source bill of material to the
disassembly bill of material with a positive quantity.
Reverts the positive quantities of all the other components to negative
quantities to be disassembled.
Create a product rework BOM
You can create and release a BOM for product rework. Using the order change
functionality, you can later add components in an order-specific routing, when
details of the product rework become clear.
3.5.2.1.2
Production Models
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Business Background
The production model provides a joint view of the manufacturing bill of material (MBOM) and the shop
floor routing and offers an alternative way of handling BOM components and shop floor production steps:
side-by-side in one screen. Currently, two types of production model are available:
Material production models for single-level materials (unitized and non-unitized)
Engineer-to-order (ETO) production models for the creation of unique products for individual customers in
a configure-to-order plus (CTO+) scenario
You release the production model to trigger downstream manufacturing processes.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Create production models
You can create a material production model by migrating a shop floor
routing with a valid MBOM assignment.
You can create an ETO production model for a preconfigured sales order
item to make customer-specific adjustments to the MBOM and shop floor
routing.
You can also create an ETO production model for a non-configurable sales
order item.
Assign production models to a change re-
cord
You can assign a production model and new versions to a new or existing
change record.
Upgrade ETO production models
You can upgrade an existing ETO production order to reflect:
Changes to the configuration of the sales order item.
A new released version of the configurable MBOM.
A new released version of the 150% shop floor routing.
Adjust BOM components
In the material production model, you can add, change, and delete BOM
components to adjust the MBOM.
In the ETO production model, you can add, change, and delete ETO ver-
sion BOM components to adjust the MBOM according to your customer’s
unique requirements. This results in an ETO version BOM.
You can also adjust the component assignments to the production steps.
Adjust production steps
You can add, change, and delete production steps in production models to
adjust the execution process according to the changed components.
You can also add, change, and delete multiple production steps.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Maintain parameter effectivity
You can maintain material production models for materials with unitized pa-
rameter effectivity. You can maintain parameter effectivity for BOM compo-
nents, operations, and operation activities and adjust component assignment
for specific effectivity ranges of the unitized material.
If required, you can create and release a production model version for a subset
of the MBOM effectivity range.
Adjust shop floor routings / ETO shop
floor routings
You can navigate to the shop floor routing (material production models) or the
ETO shop floor routing (ETO production models), where you can also adjust
the component assignments and production steps. In addition, you can update
the shop floor routing / ETO shop floor routing details such as work instruc-
tions, inspection characteristics, or PRTs.
Release production models
On completion, you release the production model.
The material production model contains the MBOM and the shop floor
routing for the material.
The ETO production model contains the 100% ETO version BOM and ETO
shop floor routing for the sales order item.
The system performs various checks to ensure the consistency of the data and
triggers downstream processes such as material requirements planning (MRP)
and production execution.
3.5.2.1.3
Manufacturing Reference Structures (MRS)
Business Background
A manufacturing reference structure (MRS) is a separate hierarchy of information that can be linked to
the BOM hierarchy to provide additional contextual information for BOM components during production
processes.
An MRS hierarchy can be defined based on a spatial, functional, or system hierarchy and it doesn't have to
match the BOM hierarchy. The link between MRS hierarchy and BOM hierarchy can provide additional context
during production processes. For example, it can help you to identify parts of a complex product in the context
of the end item, particularly if these parts are spread across a vast area.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
APIs
Through a PLM integration with SAP S/4HANA, the APIs enable you to create a
manufacturing reference structure hierarchy and link it to the BOM hierarchy.
Display manufacturing reference struc-
tures
You can display the manufacturing reference structures that have been created
by the APIs.
View MRS designator
PEO tracks MRS information for components across production engineering
and production order execution. The MRS designator is displayed alongside
other attributes of a component if the system detects a link between BOM item
and MRS item. You can view details of the MRS item to which the component is
linked within the MRS hierarchy.
Enrich functional locations
You can use Business Add-Ins to create and update functional locations based
on manufacturing reference structure information derived from as-built data.
3.5.2.1.4
Change Management
Business Background
To manage engineering changes in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations
(PEO), you use the existing Engineering Change Management (ECM) functions of SAP S/4HANA combined
with features offered by PEO.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage new or changed engineering bill
of material (EBOM) versions
You determine the correct area of responsibility for new or changed engineer-
ing bills of material so that they can be grouped in the right manufacturing
change record for initiating the manufacturing handover process.
You can search for engineering bills of material in different states and do the
following:
If available, determine the relevant change records and planning scope for
the engineering bills of material and assign them to a change record.
Navigate to the change record for further processing for the engineering
bills of material that are already assigned to a change record.
Create a change record and assign the planning scope and engineering bill
of material to it.
Create a planning scope for a new engineering bill of material and link the
engineering bill of material to it.
Automatically propose similar engineering bills of material and assign all
of them to the same change record.
Manage new or changed manufacturing
bill of material (MBOM) versions
You can search for new or changed manufacturing bills of material and assign
them to a change record. You can also search for manufacturing bills of mate-
rial that are already assigned to a change record and navigate to the change
record for further processing. You can create a change record and assign the
manufacturing bills of material to it.
Manage engineering changes using exter-
nal engineering snapshots and manufac-
turing snapshots
Through a PLM integration with SAP S/4HANA, you can package engineering
or manufacturing data from your external system and send it to your PEO
system. APIs then map the data from the packages into snapshots, which
can be either engineering or manufacturing snapshots, depending on whether
you're incorporating engineering-driven or manufacturing-driven changes.
Snapshots facilitate the change management process for handling product
structures and additional information such as 3D visuals, product manufactur-
ing information (PMI), or manufacturing process definitions (MPDs) from an
external system.
When working with engineering snapshots, you can prepare the necessary
data for the EBOM-to-MBOM handover, for example, when manufacturing a
material in a specific plant for the first time. The system creates or updates the
change record and adds the EBOM versions, external engineering snapshot,
and planning scope to the change record.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Manage engineering changes using engi-
neering change notice driven snapshots
Through a PLM integration with SAP S/4HANA, you can package engineering
or manufacturing data from your external system and send it to your PEO
system. If these engineering packages have no snapshot information (that is,
no hierarchical EBOM information), APIs can map the engineering data into en-
gineering change notices. Based on the engineering change notice, production
engineers can prepare the manufacturing engineering (MFG) change record
with the planning scope, header EBOM, and in this case, an internal engineer-
ing snapshot.
When working with engineering change notices, you can prepare the necessary
data for the EBOM-to-MBOM handover, for example, when manufacturing a
material in a specific plant for the first time. The system creates or updates the
change record and adds the EBOM versions, internal engineering snapshot,
and planning scope to the change record.
Using manufacturing snapshots to create
shop floor routings, rework routings, and
reference templates
If the manufacturing snapshot contains a manufacturing process definition
(MPD) file only and no MBOM, you can create the following directly from the
change record, depending on the routing type maintained in the PiP (Produc-
tion Integration Portal) source file:
Shop floor routings
Rework routings
Reference operation sets
Reference operations
Reference operation activity groups
Reference operation activities
On receipt of a new manufacturing snapshot revision, you can also create
new versions of the rework routings and reference templates to perform the
upgrade.
Monitor snapshots
You can monitor the engineering and manufacturing snapshots for the prod-
ucts you're manufacturing. For example, you can view the key data and as-
sociated change record for each snapshot revision and check whether the
production integration portal (PiP) data and change record were prepared suc-
cessfully. If necessary, you can trigger the preparation process again manually.
You can also create a new manufacturing snapshot revision and link it to the
relevant MBOM version in cases where the previous MBOM version has a man-
ufacturing snapshot assigned to it, and the subsequent MBOM version does
not.
Create a new target shop floor routing us-
ing a source shop floor routing
In the change record, you can create a new target shop floor routing for an
MBOM by copying an already existing source shop floor routing. During the
copy process, the system maps the MBOM components and instances of the
new target MBOM to the operation activities of the referenced source shop
floor routing.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Analyze change impact
The change impact analysis helps you to find different manufacturing-related
objects that may be affected by a change to an existing object such as an
engineering bill of material, manufacturing bill of material, or routing. After
the related objects are determined, you can take some quick action such as
putting the production order on hold. You can also add the objects to the
change record to take action on them later in the context of the change cycle.
When working with a unitized bill of material and shop floor routing, you can
see the effectivity impacted by the change.
If you work with snapshots and a snapshot contains product manufacturing
information (PMI) data or a manufacturing process definition (MPD), you can
determine routings impacted by PMI or MPD changes.
Compare bill of material changes
You can compare two bills of material and display a net change to understand
what exactly has been changed between BOM versions. This is possible for
both EBOMs and MBOMs.
The net change of an BOM version displays the delta between the given version
and its previous version. This gives you an idea of the scope of the change
that has taken place in the BOM, such as changes of BOM components and
changes in component quantities.
3.5.2.1.5
Production Integration Portal (PiP)
Business Background
The production integration portal (PiP) enables you to integrate various kinds of files from an external system
to SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO) and subsequently use
these source files in production engineering when creating shop floor routings, rework routings, and reference
templates.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Create a mapping between PiP data and
the target objects in PEO
In Customizing, you can define the source file classes required to create the
mappings between the source items of the PiP source files you receive from
your external system (such as a PLM system) and the target objects that are
required to create the shop floor routing, rework routing, or reference template
in PEO.
For more complex mapping scenarios, you can also implement BAdIs.
Import PiP source files
You can import engineering and manufacturing data from an external system
into the SAP S/4HANA system using a PLM integration solution. The PEO
system creates a snapshot which can be either an engineering snapshot or
a manufacturing snapshot. Depending on your system's configuration, the
system transforms and converts the PiP source data to the required internal
format for saving it to the PiP database tables in PEO and creates a change
record with the information available in the snapshot.
Monitor snapshots
You can monitor snapshots of the type engineering and manufacturing, iden-
tify snapshots for which issues exist, and trigger this process again manually, if
necessary.
Use snapshots for change management
You can use snapshots to facilitate the change management process for han-
dling product structures and additional information such as 3D visuals, prod-
uct manufacturing information (PMI), or manufacturing process definitions
(MPDs) from an external system.
Create shop floor routings, rework rout-
ings, and reference templates using man-
ufacturing snapshots
If a manufacturing snapshot exists for an MBOM, you create the shop floor
routing using the snapshot. This controls the availability of the PiP workspace
where you process the PiP source files contained in the snapshot:
MPD: You can hand over the source items of an MPD file to the shop
floor routing structure. You can perform an initial handover and you can
upgrade to a new revision of an MPD file and perform the delta handover.
PMI: You can create routing objects using source PMI data. You can also
upgrade to a new revision of the PMI file and automatically hand over the
changes.
If no MBOM is associated with the manufacturing snapshot but only an MPD
file, you can use it to create either a rework routing or a reference template
depending on the routing type defined in the MPD file.
Automated handover
You can create an advanced workflow that can be triggered automatically to
perform the handover steps required to create a shop floor routing and pro-
duction version using data from an external system.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Migrate legacy routings from an external
system to PEO shop floor routings
You can migrate your legacy shop floor routings from an external source to
PEO shop floor routings using the existing integration features available with
the production integration portal (PiP).
3.5.2.1.6
Shop Floor Routing Management
Business Background
During the production engineering phase, you design and continuously improve manufacturing equipment,
production facilities, and manufacturing procedures. This process enables you to model a detailed execution
plan including required quality steps.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available for managing shop floor routings in SAP S/4HANA
Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO):
Key Feature
Use
Manage version-controlled shop floor
routings
You can create multiple routing versions for a routing group and counter. Each
routing version has its own release status. Released routing versions can be
used in production execution and can therefore no longer be changed. If you
want to make changes to a released routing version, you create a new version.
You can also copy a released version and make the necessary changes. You
can't use the new routing version in production execution as long as it is in
process. This procedure guarantees consistency in production execution.
Create shop floor routings
You can create shop floor routings:
With MBOM assignment: You can create and release a shop floor routing
for one material and MBOM.
Without MBOM assignment: You can create a shop floor routing version
and assign a material to it without assigning any MBOM.
With multiple MBOM assignment: Depending on the version profile, you
can create a shop floor routing and assign multiple MBOMs to it.
With manufacturing snapshot assignment: If a manufacturing snapshot
exists for an MBOM, you create the shop floor routing using the snapshot.
This triggers the availability of the PiP workspace where you use the
source files of the snapshot to create the shop floor routing.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Enable flexible processing
You can activate flexible processing mode when creating a routing to eliminate
dependencies between operations during production execution in the following
way:
In serialized execution, no consistency checks on operation sequence are
performed. This means operators can process operations in any sequence
and work on operations in parallel.
In non-serialized execution, no cross-operation quantity and status
checks are performed. This means operators can process operations in
any order and post any quantities.
In addition, you can define relationships between operation activities in differ-
ent operations.
Create parallel sequences
You can create sequences that you can use to schedule the order’s operations
to run in parallel, in same way as in classic production orders.
Enable execution in SAP Manufacturing
Execution (SAP ME)
For non-configurable and non-unitized products, you can enable integration
with SAP ME if the execution tasks for a shop floor routing are to be performed
in SAP ME and not in the execution apps available in Manufacturing for produc-
tion engineering and operations.
Create and manage workflow
You may have a team of several production engineers working on one shop
floor routing version simultaneously. This work has to be carefully coordinated
to ensure all tasks are completed. You can manage such coordination tasks
using the workflow features. If necessary, you can change a workflow that has
already started and define that several steps can be processed in parallel.
Model a detailed production process
A shop floor routing provides a very detailed description of the operations
and operation activities required to produce a material. In addition, the routing
contains details about the work centers at which the operations and operation
activities are processed and the manufacturing bill of material components
that are required.
You use operation activities to model the steps to be performed in production
with a high level of detail. You assign each operation activity a status and action
schema (SAS) that defines the statuses and possible transitions allowed for
the operation activity. You can use any-order groups to define sets of opera-
tion activities that can be completed in any sequence or simultaneous groups
which allow the parallel processing of operation activities in production execu-
tion.
You can create and change operations and operation activities individually or
use mass creation and changing options to speed up the process.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Model a detailed production process for
unitized products
The effectivity information is derived from the assigned manufacturing bill of
material (MBOM) and passed on to the routing header. The operations inherit
the effectivity information from the routing header and the operation activities
inherit the effectivity information from the operations. You can change the
effectivity for operations and operation activities, as necessary.
You can change the effectivity information at routing header level meaning that
you can create a routing version for a subset of the effectivity range defined in
the MBOM. In addition, the MBOM assigned to the routing version may have an
open effectivity range or an undefined effectivity parameter (wildcard *). That
is, no upper limit has been defined.
You can define an effectivity filter to create a separate work environment for
a limited effective range. You can change the operations, operation activities,
and components for this work environment without these changes affecting
the rest of the routing.
You can avoid having to split operation activities when working with alternative
components that are relevant for different effectivity ranges by using logical
components.
Model a detailed production process for
configurable materials
You can create shop floor routings for configurable products or materials.
Often operation activities are only needed for a certain configuration of the
final product. Shop floor routing management allows you to define if and how
an operation activity depends on configurable components.
Adjust engineer-to-order shop floor rout-
ings (ETO shop floor routings)
From the ETO production model, you can navigate to the ETO shop floor rout-
ing where you can adjust the production steps and component assignments
as well as the shop floor routing details such as work instructions, inspection
characteristics, or PRTs.
Create and manage reference templates
for shop floor routings
You can create and manage reference templates (operation sets, operations,
operation activity groups, and operation activities) that you can insert into
shop floor routings, rework routings, and order-specific shop floor routings.
Reference templates are version based for which a complete lifecycle manage-
ment is enabled. Releasing a version enables its use in routings. If you need to
make changes to a released reference template, you first create a new version,
make your changes, and release the new version. Where-used information is
available to find and upgrade all routings that are affected by a new version
quickly and efficiently.
You can also create reference templates using the production integration por-
tal (PiP). In this case, the manufacturing snapshot only contains a manufactur-
ing process definition (MPD) file and no MBOM and the change management
process is controlled by manufacturing snapshot revisions.
You can find the released reference templates in the template library, in the
shared templates folders. You can assign classification data to reference tem-
plates to enable sophisticated search possibilities.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Create and manage shared copy tem-
plates
The shared templates folder in the template library also contains shared copy
templates that are available to all users. Shared copy templates have no lifecy-
cle management and are not version based.
You can assign classification data to the shared copy templates of the types
operation, operation activity group, and operation activity to enable sophisti-
cated search possibilities.
Create and manage private copy tem-
plates
If you have objects that you reuse regularly such as operations, PRTs, inspec-
tion characteristics, or documents, you can copy them to your private copy
templates folder in the template library.
You can also set links to reference templates and shared copy templates in
your private folders.
You can access your private copy templates in all routings and reuse these
objects as often as necessary.
Assign a change record to the routing
You can assign one or more manufacturing change records to the shop floor
routing and rework routing. Depending on the settings defined in the version
profile, it may only be possible to release a routing if a change record assign-
ment exists.
Assign operations to an external service
provider
You can specify that selected operations are to be performed by an external
service provider. In this case, the system creates a purchase requisition to
order and pay for the service provider's services.
Set a change alert count
You can enter a change alert count for each operation activity when maintain-
ing the routing version. A change alert notifies the operator in production
about engineering changes and highlights such changes. By setting a counter
in the operation activity, you can stipulate that receipt of this change informa-
tion has to be acknowledged once or several times. This ensures that engineer-
ing changes are visible and correctly implemented.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Create work instructions for operation ac-
tivities (also using standard texts)
You can create detailed work instructions for operation activities that are made
available to the production operator on the shop floor before starting the
activity. For example, you can:
Create or reuse standard texts and images. That is, you can reference or
copy existing standard texts or images into work instructions thus reduc-
ing the effort involved in creating and maintaining work instructions.
Create work instructions with interactive data collection so that produc-
tion operators on the shop floor can follow complex instructions and
record data within the context of the instruction.
Add and edit reference links to single or multiple components as well as to
inspection characteristics and PRTs.
Create 3D views showing specific angles or areas of the 3D visual if the
MBOM assigned to the shop floor routing has instances and if a 3D visual
is available. You can also link the new 3D views to the work instructions.
Incorporate reference designators or MRS designators (manufacturing
reference structure) as a reference into non-interactive and interactive
work instructions.
Create bookmarks in the work instructions to make internal navigation
more efficient when the work instruction text is long. You can also create
bookmarks in standard texts and reuse them in work instructions.
Print work instructions in the shop floor routing.
Assign components to operation activities
You can assign components to the operation activities for which they are re-
quired. You can also split MBOM item quantities and assign partial quantities
to different operation activities of an operation. The assignment to operations
is then done implicitly by the system and this information is displayed accord-
ingly. The assignment at operation activity level is relevant for execution.
When working with component instances, you can take account of reference
designator information when assigning component instances to operation ac-
tivities.
In a disassembly scenario, you can assign components with a negative quantity
(by-products) to operation activities.
If the MBOM associated with the shop floor routing contains alternative item
groups, all alternative components are implicitly assigned to the operation
activity when you assign the primary component.
Define assembly sequence for compo-
nents
You can determine the order in which components assigned to the same oper-
ation activity are to be assembled in production execution.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Assign inspection characteristics to oper-
ation activities
You can, for example:
Assign inspection characteristics or tables of inspection characteristics to
operation activities which describe the inspection criteria.
Assign reference master inspection characteristics for which statistical
process control (SPC) is enabled.
Specify a component for which the inspection characteristic or table of
inspection characteristics is to be performed. The component can be any
component of the MBOM irrespective of its operation activity assignment.
If no component is specified in the inspection characteristic, the data is
collected for the header material.
Specify any required test equipment that is required to perform the in-
spection.
Assign input processing to inspection characteristics.
Assign an inspection method to inspection characteristics
Define that a quality notification is to be created automatically should the
inspection results not conform to predefined measurements.
Define extended quality checks
You can mark operation activities and inspection characteristics as relevant for
extended quality checks (EQC), for example, to validate a new process or com-
ply with special regulations. By assigning EQC groups to operation activities
and inspection characteristics, you also have the option to split a full EQC into
partial quality checks.
Assign production resources/tools (PRTs)
to operation activities
You can assign PRTs to operation activities and define whether the use of a
tool is to be registered in execution. This information is made available to the
production operator on the shop floor before starting the activity.
Assign documents to the shop floor rout-
ing header
You can assign header documents to the shop floor routing header containing
information that is applicable to all the operation activities in the shop floor
routing.
Assign documents to operations
You can assign operation documents containing information that is applicable
to all the operation activities of a specific operation.
Assign documents to operation activities
You can assign OA documents to operation activities which are made available
to the production operator on the shop floor.
Assign required qualifications to operation
activities
You can assign required qualifications to operation activities. You can use quali-
fications to ensure that only qualified production operators perform critical or
difficult operation activities. HR qualifications are assigned to the production
operators and the system checks that the qualification requirements are fulfil-
led when the operator starts the operation activity.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Assign buyoff cycles to operation activities You can assign buyoff cycles to operation activities. A buyoff is a confirmation
that an operation activity was performed as defined in the routing.
When maintaining the operation activity, you can create a new buyoff sequence
or you can assign a predefined buyoff cycle template. A buyoff cycle template
defines all the required experts who have to check the quality of an operation
activity. If a buyoff cycle template has been assigned to an operation activity,
this activity cannot be completed in production execution until all buyoffs have
been fulfilled.
You can also assign HR qualifications to buyoff steps to control who can ap-
prove the buyoff steps at production completion.
Hand over PiP data to routing target ob-
jects
If certain prerequisites are fulfilled, you can assign product manufacturing
information (PMI) data to routing target objects such as inspection character-
istics, PRTs, or work instructions, for example.
You can also use a manufacturing process definition (MPD) to create and
update the shop floor routing.
If a new version of the source file is available, you can compare the current
version with the new version and hand over the changes.
Manage action handlers for operation ac-
tivities
You can display available action handlers and their parameters and parameter
values. You can also add further action handlers specifically for a particular
routing, for example, to implement additional checks when a user performs an
action.
Extended message log
You can check the routing consistency using an extended message log. The
system displays all errors and inconsistencies that you have to solve to release
the routing. You can click each error to navigate to the error source where you
can fix the problem.
Compare routing versions
You can use the comparison mode to see the differences between two routing
versions. This means that you can easily manage all the changes that have
been made from version to version and use this past information to update
the current version as necessary. For example, you can compare different
versions of one shop floor routing, or of a master shop floor routing version
and an order-specific routing version. Options are also available for comparing
unitized parameter effectivity.
In the comparison mode, you can continue to work on the routing structure.
Most of the functions available in non-comparison mode are also available in
comparison mode. In addition, you can perform the release check, release the
routing version, and create the production version from the comparison mode.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Create rework routings
You can create a rework routing, which is required in execution if a defect is
recorded in production for which a rework task is created.
You can add as many rework operations as needed and operation activities
for rework. There can be multiple rework routings per plant, and each rework
routing can be linked to a particular defect.
You can also create rework routings using the production integration portal
(PiP). In this case, the manufacturing snapshot only contains a manufacturing
process definition (MPD) file and no MBOM.
Create product rework routings
You can create a product rework routing to define the operations to be per-
formed on a product as part of product rework. Using the order change func-
tionality, you can later add more operations in an order-specific routing, when
details of the product rework become clear.
Track changes using the change history
You can view a table of all the changes that were done to all types of routings
and reference templates. The change history is available during maintenance
as well as after release for reference purposes.
Maintain production versions
The production version determines the production techniques according to
which a material is to be manufactured. A material may have several manufac-
turing bills of material (MBOMs) and several routings used for its production.
You define which MBOM and which routing combination is to be used in the
production version. It is possible to use different master data for planning and
execution purposes.
You can create and release a production version for a shop floor routing that
only has a material assigned to it (no MBOM assignment).
3.5.2.1.7
Production Engineering and Planning
Business Background
The production engineering and planning process is crucial to manufacturers operating in a competitive
environment. SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO) supports a range
of process variants, which form the basis for the production engineering and planning process through to
production execution. This gives you the flexibility to adopt a production engineering and planning approach
that best suits your requirements.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Create planning bills of material
You can create a planning bill of material for planning purposes before comple-
tion of the final shop floor master data. This enables you to produce or procure
component materials with long lead times, for example. Planning BOMs sup-
port phantom assemblies.
Create planning routings
You can create simplified planning routings that are used with the planning
bill of material for planning purposes before completion of the final shop floor
master data. This enables you to produce materials with long lead times. You
can also assign known bottleneck work centers to operations so that work
center capacity can be planned well in advance. Planning routings can be date
effective or parameter effective. Planning routings can be assigned to a change
record and they support phantom assemblies.
Check readiness of shop floor master data
for production
You can easily check for issues with shop floor master data that prevent the
planned order from being converted to a production order. Several options are
available for resolving these issues.
3.5.2.2
Extended Production Operations
3.5.2.2.1
Production Control
Business Background
This process enables you to manage and regulate the manufacturing process using the features available in
SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO). It is typically performed by the
production supervisor who monitors production progress and reacts to issues such as missing components,
delays, holds, or quality issues.
Key Features
The following table explains the key features available:
Key Feature
Use
Manage work load for production opera-
tors
You can view detailed information on your production operators and manage
their work load.
Read production order master data
You can update the production order with the latest version of the bill of mate-
rials and shop floor routing before releasing it.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Update routing version
You can update the routing version in one or more shop floor orders based on
the order explosion date or a new explosion date. The orders to be updated
must still have a master routing (not an order-specific routing) and operation
activities affected by the update must not have been started yet in the order.
Change dates and quantities for a produc-
tion order
You can change basic dates and quantities for a production order.
Release production orders/operations
You can trigger the production process by releasing the production order or its
operations.
Determine that an order is relevant for ex-
tended quality checks (EQC)
When creating a shop floor order, you can define that additional quality checks
are required. This could be required, for example, to validate a new process.
You can define these additional EQC relevant indicators at IC or operation ac-
tivity level and they are included in the shop floor order if EQC is also selected
for the order. Otherwise, the system filters them out. If all operation activities in
one operation are filtered out, the system also filters out the operation.
Exchange components in an alternative
item group
You can exchange the primary component of an alternative item group for one
of the alternative components.
Exchange components for a global alter-
native
You can exchange an order component for a global alternative (such as a
follow-up material defined in the material master), without having to update
the shop floor BOM.
Put objects on hold and release them
again
You can temporarily block production by placing orders, materials, operations,
or work centers on hold. When placing the object on hold, you can specify a
reason code and note. Once the issue has been solved, you can release the
hold. You have the tools you need to monitor all objects that are currently on
hold as well as holds that have been released.
Reprocess materials movements
You can view failed material movements to determine why they failed. To help
you fix issues, you can use mass update functions to replace missing or incor-
rect values, or you can use navigation options to branch to the material and
make changes, for example. You can then reprocess the material movement.
Incorporate order changes
You can effectively incorporate order changes at any time during the manufac-
turing process, that is, even when orders are already in process. By creating
an order-specific routing, you can handle issues more effectively by making
ad hoc changes to a specific order without changing the master routing and
manufacturing bill of materials.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Trigger disassembly due to an engineering
change
You can trigger a disassembly scenario to incorporate engineering changes
that require the disassembly of components. That is, a new manufacturing bill
of material (MBOM) version exists that you want to use for a production order
that is currently in execution. To make the changes, you create an order-spe-
cific shop floor routing where you can, for example:
Compare the old and new MBOM versions in a dedicated comparison
screen.
Permanently disassemble components to be replaced and define a stock
type and storage location where the disassembled component is to be
placed.
Temporarily disassemble and reassemble components that have to be
removed to disassemble another component.
The production order is updated when you save and release the order-specific
routing.
Trigger disassembly due to rework
You can trigger a disassembly scenario to handle rework that requires the
disassembly of components. In this case, a production operator records a
defect for which you create a follow-up action to change the order. When you
save the follow-up action, the system puts the order on hold and creates an
order-specific shop floor routing where you can, for example:
Add new or modify existing operations and operation activities if they are
editable.
Create, change, or delete components if they are editable.
Disassemble components whereby disassembly may be permanent or
temporary.
Define a stock type and storage location where a component is to be
placed after permanent disassembly.
The production order is updated when you save and release the order-specific
routing.
Split orders
You can split a shop floor order into two separate orders with the same mate-
rial if you need to handle the processed partial quantity separately from the
remaining quantity to be produced. An order split is allowed at operation level,
which means the serial numbers (for a serialized product) or partial order
quantity (for a non-serialized product) must have completed all operation ac-
tivities in the current operation and be in queue at the operation from which
you want to split the order.
Create product rework orders
You can create a shop floor order to rework any serialized product that has
been produced in-house or manufactured externally. Product rework enables
you to replace defective or degraded components identified for completed
orders, as well as perform repairs or maintenance on products that have been
in storage or returned from the field.
SAP S/4HANA Enhanced LoB Functions

---

Key Feature
Use
Monitor production order progress
You have up-to-date information regarding the processing status of all orders
focusing on exception situations.
Monitor production operation progress
You have up-to-date information regarding the processing status of all opera-
tions focusing on exception situations. You can also navigate to the operation
activity view of the operations. In addition you can distribute the work load by
assigning production operators to the operation activities.
Monitor and track production
